name: Deer
description: Skittish prey animal. Flees at the first sign of danger. Useful as food, not as a fight.

sensory.sight: brown shapes between trees, alert ears, the white flash of a tail as they bolt
sensory.sound: the snap of a twig, then hoofbeats retreating through undergrowth
sensory.smell: nothing — you smell them on the wind only if downwind
sensory.feel: stillness, then sudden movement, then emptiness

encounter_hooks: fresh tracks at a stream, still drinking when you round the bend
a clearing with a stag standing motionless, watching you
antler-scraped bark on the trees, marking territory
notes: Deer are atmosphere, not encounters. Their presence means the forest is healthy. Their absence means something's wrong. A stag watching you from a clearing is a moment, not a fight.
